Scenario.title: The Oldsmar Treatment Plant Intrusion Scenario
Scenario.overview: Communications Simple The threat actors exploited human error and system vulnerabilities to gain unauthorized access to a treatment plant's SCADA system and manipulate setpoints for a specific chemical.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Authentication issues

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-6448 [CWE-22]: Unspecified vulnerability in Microsoft Outlook allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a crafted message.
stage.description: The threat actors sent a targeted email with a malicious attachment to an employee at the treatment plant, exploiting their lack of security awareness.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2020-3118 [CWE-749]: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting') in Microsoft SharePoint Server allows remote attackers to inject arbitrary web script or HTML via unspecified vectors.
stage.description: The malicious file from the phishing email was copied to the victim's machine, allowing the malware to execute.

stage.name: Impact
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.description: The attacker manipulated setpoints for a specific chemical by accessing the SCADA system, similar to the Oldsmar Treatment Plant Intrusion.

Scenario.summary: The Oldsmar Treatment Plant Intrusion scenario demonstrates a simple yet effective attack chain where threat actors exploited human error and system vulnerabilities to gain unauthorized access to a treatment plant's SCADA system and manipulate setpoints for a specific chemical.
